title: Orlando 3-Day Family Vacation
metaTitle: Orlando 3-Day Family Vacation | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Orlando, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Walt Disney World Magic Kingdom and Epcot. Prices from $708 per person (estimate).
highlights: Universal's Hollywood Rip Ride Rockit and thrill coasters
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Orlando, United States — Humid subtropical climate with hot, rainy, thunderstorm-prone summers (Jun-Sep) and mild, dry winters (Dec-Feb) with occasional cold snaps. January to May, outside spring break weeks, for milder weather, lower humidity, and shorter theme park lines. This itinerary blends Walt Disney World Magic Kingdom, Epcot, Disney Springs with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Walt Disney World Magic Kingdom, Epcot, Disney Springs
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Orlando
itinerary.description: Land at Orlando International Airport (MCO) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Walt Disney World Magic Kingdom — Flagship Disney theme park. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Orlando: Epcot & Universal Studios Florida
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Epcot — Future World and World Showcase pavilions. In the afternoon, continue to Universal Studios Florida — Movie and TV-themed rides and shows. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Universal's Islands of Adventure or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MCO for your departure flight.

travelTips: Use Disney Genie+/Lightning Lane and Universal Express systems to cut wait times
Book park tickets and dining reservations well in advance, especially around holidays
Ride outdoor attractions early or late to avoid the hottest, most crowded midday hours
Download park apps for real-time wait times and mobile ordering
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable walking shoes for long theme park days
Sunscreen and a refillable water bottle for hot, humid weather
A light rain poncho for frequent afternoon summer storms
A portable phone charger for all-day park app use
thingsToDo: Winter Park Scenic Boat Tour area
Orlando Farmers Market at Lake Eola
Universal's Hollywood Rip Ride Rockit and thrill coasters
Kennedy Space Center astronaut training experience
thingsToAvoid: Visiting during major US school holiday weeks if you dislike crowds
Underestimating the size of the parks and amount of walking involved
Skipping required park reservations alongside ticket purchases at Disney
topRestaurants: The Boathouse Disney Springs — Seafood and steak ($$$)
Victoria & Albert's — Fine dining tasting menu ($$$$)
Chef Art Smith's Homecomin' — Southern comfort food ($$)
Toothsome Chocolate Emporium — American dessert and dining ($$)
adventureActivities: Universal's Hollywood Rip Ride Rockit and thrill coasters
Kennedy Space Center astronaut training experience
luxuryExperiences: Stay at a Disney Deluxe Villa or Four Seasons Orlando
Private VIP tour guide at Universal or Disney
Private fireworks viewing cruise on the Seven Seas Lagoon
honeymoonExperiences: Romantic dinner at California Grill overlooking Magic Kingdom fireworks
Couples spa at a Disney or Four Seasons resort
Private Segway tour of Epcot's World Showcase
Sunset cruise on Lake Buena Vista
budgetExperiences: Free entry and browsing at Disney Springs
Lake Eola Park picnic and swan boats
localEtiquette: Tipping 18-20% is standard at sit-down restaurants.
Tipping housekeeping and valet is customary at hotels.
Queue politely for rides and respect the Lightning Lane/Genie+ systems.
Casual dress is the norm everywhere, including most restaurants.
Sales tax is added at checkout and not included in listed prices.
localTransportGuide: Renting a car is the most practical way to get around Orlando's sprawling, highway-linked attractions; Disney offers free buses, monorail, and Skyliner gondolas between its own parks and resorts. Uber and Lyft are widely used for trips outside Disney property.
bestTimeToVisit: January to May, outside spring break weeks, for milder weather, lower humidity, and shorter theme park lines.
weather: Humid subtropical climate with hot, rainy, thunderstorm-prone summers (Jun-Sep) and mild, dry winters (Dec-Feb) with occasional cold snaps.
visaInformation: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries (UK, EU, Australia, Japan, and others) need an approved ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa.
averageFlightTime: ~15.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Orlando?
faqs.answer: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries (UK, EU, Australia, Japan, and others) need an approved ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Orlando?
faqs.answer: January to May, outside spring break weeks, for milder weather, lower humidity, and shorter theme park lines. Humid subtropical climate with hot, rainy, thunderstorm-prone summers (Jun-Sep) and mild, dry winters (Dec-Feb) with occasional cold snaps.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Orlando safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Orlando is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Orlando?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Magic Kingdom.

reviews.author: Omar Silva
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Celebration Main Street and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Elena Costa
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Epcot and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mia Patel
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to ICON Park Wheel and Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Ibrahim
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Universal Studios Florida alone was worth the trip, but combined with CityWalk Universal Orlando this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Liam Sharma
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Our trip to Orlando was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Epcot was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
